title: Informational Interviews and Reality Testing · Hiring Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A hiring panel compares near-miss decisions for informational interviews and reality testing and accepts only role-relevant, truthful, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for consented role-reality interview study. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define a concrete uncertainty about tasks, environment, entry routes, evidence, or tradeoffs that a practitioner can help test.
Research public context and explain why this person may have relevant perspective without presuming identity, availability, or representation.
Plan a short accessible conversation with neutral behavior questions, time consent, recording consent, and an easy stopping path.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Learning Question · changed case · Quiz Learning Question
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A nominal learning conversation hides a referral request, asks generic questions, records without consent, and generalizes one practitioner view to a whole market. The quiz variation 2 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

Which action demonstrates Learning Question without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Learning Question · changed case · Quiz Learning Question.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Action Feedback
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Action Feedback: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A nominal learning conversation hides a referral request, asks generic questions, records without consent, and generalizes one practitioner view to a whole market. The quiz variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Action Feedback.
steps.why: Retrieving Action Feedback through the concrete consented role-reality interview study case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Action Feedback, keep Action Feedback connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Close the loop by acting on one bounded insight, reporting the result when welcome, and revising the role hypothesis from combined evidence.
